content_en:
Al-Isabah was recognized as the definitive reference work on the Companions of the Prophet almost immediately after its composition. Its combination of comprehensive coverage, critical rigor, and reliable determination of who was and was not a Companion gave it an authority that no previous work on the subject had achieved. Subsequent scholars who needed to determine the status of a specific individual invariably consulted al-Isabah first, and its judgments were treated as authoritative unless compelling contrary evidence was adduced.

The work's influence on Islamic scholarship extended across multiple disciplines. In hadith sciences, al-Isabah is essential for establishing the status of narrators at the level of the Companions, which is the first link in every chain of transmission. In Islamic history, it is the primary biographical source for the generation of Muslims who knew the Prophet personally. In Islamic law, the determination of Companionship bears on questions of the acceptability of narrations and the weight of early opinions.

The work became a standard part of the curriculum in institutions specializing in hadith sciences. Students studying ilm ar-rijal — the science of narrator evaluation — could not complete their training without mastering al-Isabah alongside the other biographical works of Ibn Hajar. In the traditional scholarly hierarchy, proficiency in al-Isabah was a mark of advanced training.

Modern scholarship on early Islamic history and the formation of the hadith corpus relies heavily on al-Isabah. Historians studying the first Muslim community, the transmission of the Prophetic traditions, and the social history of early Arabia and the conquered territories have found it indispensable. Its detailed entries provide evidence for demographic, social, and religious questions that no other source addresses with comparable breadth and critical rigor. The range of regions, backgrounds, and circumstances represented among the Companions documented in al-Isabah also makes it a valuable resource for appreciating the diversity of the early Muslim community: men and women from Arabia, Persia, Ethiopia, and the Byzantine world are all represented, reflecting the universal reach of the Prophet's message and the varied human materials from which the first Muslim community was formed.
content_id:
Al-Isabah diakui sebagai karya referensi definitif tentang Para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ hampir segera setelah penyelesaiannya. Kombinasinya antara cakupan komprehensif, ketelitian kritis, dan penentuan yang dapat diandalkan tentang siapa yang merupakan dan bukan Sahabat memberikannya otoritas yang tidak dicapai oleh karya-karya terdahulu tentang subjek ini. Ulama-ulama berikutnya yang perlu menentukan status individu tertentu secara tak terelakkan berkonsultasi dengan Al-Isabah terlebih dahulu, dan penilaian-penilaiannya diperlakukan sebagai otoritatif kecuali ada bukti yang meyakinkan sebaliknya.

Pengaruh karya ini pada beasiswa Islam meluas ke berbagai disiplin ilmu. Dalam ilmu-ilmu hadis, Al-Isabah sangat penting untuk menetapkan status perawi di tingkat Para Sahabat, yang merupakan mata rantai pertama dalam setiap sanad. Dalam sejarah Islam, karya ini adalah sumber biografi utama untuk generasi Muslim yang mengenal Nabi ﷺ secara pribadi. Dalam hukum Islam, penentuan status Sahabat berkaitan dengan pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang dapat diterimanya riwayat-riwayat dan bobot pendapat-pendapat awal.

Karya ini menjadi bagian standar dari kurikulum di lembaga-lembaga yang mengkhususkan diri dalam ilmu-ilmu hadis. Para pelajar yang mempelajari ilmu ar-rijal — ilmu evaluasi perawi — tidak dapat menyelesaikan pelatihan mereka tanpa menguasai Al-Isabah bersama karya-karya biografi lain dari Ibn Hajar. Dalam hierarki keilmuan tradisional, kemahiran dalam Al-Isabah adalah tanda pelatihan lanjutan.

Beasiswa modern tentang sejarah Islam awal dan pembentukan korpus hadis sangat bergantung pada Al-Isabah. Para sejarahwan yang mempelajari komunitas Muslim pertama, transmisi tradisi-tradisi kenabian, dan sejarah sosial Arabia awal serta wilayah-wilayah yang ditaklukkan telah menemukan karya ini sangat diperlukan. Entri-entri rincinya menyediakan bukti untuk pertanyaan-pertanyaan demografis, sosial, dan agama yang tidak ada sumber lain yang membahasnya dengan keluasan dan ketelitian kritis yang sebanding. Rentang wilayah, latar belakang, dan keadaan yang terwakili di antara para entri Al-Isabah menawarkan gambaran unik tentang komunitas Muslim awal sebagai sebuah fenomena sosial global, tidak hanya sebagai komunitas keagamaan.
content_ar:
Al-Isabah was recognized as the definitive reference work on the Companions of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم almost immediately after its composition. Its combination of comprehensive coverage, critical rigor, and reliable determination of who was and was not a Companion gave it an authority that no previous work on the subject had achieved. Subsequent scholars who needed to determine the status of a specific individual invariably consulted al-Isabah first, and its judgments were treated as authoritative unless compelling contrary evidence was adduced.

The work's influence on Islamic scholarship extended across multiple disciplines. In hadith sciences, al-Isabah is essential for establishing the status of narrators at the level of the Companions, which is the first link in every chain of transmission. In Islamic history, it is the primary biographical source for the generation of Muslims who knew النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم personally. In Islamic law, the determination of Companionship bears on questions of the acceptability of narrations and the weight of early opinions.

The work became a standard part of the curriculum in institutions specializing in hadith sciences. Students studying ilm ar-rijal — the science of narrator evaluation — could not complete their training without mastering al-Isabah alongside the other biographical works of Ibn Hajar. In the traditional scholarly hierarchy, proficiency in al-Isabah was a mark of advanced training.

Modern scholarship on early Islamic history and the formation of the hadith corpus relies heavily on al-Isabah. Historians studying the first Muslim community, the transmission of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلمic traditions, and the social history of early Arabia and the conquered territories have found it indispensable. Its detailed entries provide evidence for demographic, social, and religious questions that no other source addresses with comparable breadth and critical rigor. The range of regions, backgrounds, and circumstances represented among the Companions documented in al-Isabah also makes it a valuable resource for appreciating the diversity of the early Muslim community: men and women from Arabia, Persia, Ethiopia, and the Byzantine world are all represented, reflecting the universal reach of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم's message and the varied human materials from which the first Muslim community was formed.
